instruction.title: Pricing Logic & Financial Engine Architecture
instruction.overview: This document defines the pricing compilation pipeline, precision guidelines, decoupled providers, locale formatting, and caching policies.

precision_rules.rule: No binary floats for calculations (avoids display drift, e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 === 0.30000000000000004).
All price math operations must be performed using integer minor units (Cents/Paise).
Division back to major currency units (Dollars/Euros/Rupees) occurs strictly in the formatting layer.

regional_multiplier_strategy.notes: Regional multipliers represent PPP adjustments and are configurable inside pricing.config.ts.

formatter_strategy.rules: Decouple display currency preferences from browser locale contexts.
formatter_strategy.example: An Indian developer (locale en-IN) viewing the page can select USD currency. Display formats as $29.00 but respects local spacing punctuation.

implementations.production: Fetches live rates from an external financial API.
implementations.hackathon_static: Resolves fixed rates matching pricing.config.ts defaults.
implementations.fallback: If rate resolutions fail or return <= 0, fall back to multiplier 1.0 (USD).

caching_and_validation.validation: Engine validates active currency codes against a whitelist ('USD', 'INR', 'EUR'). Invalid inputs reject to USD.
caching_and_validation.clamping: Rate multipliers are clamped to a minimum factor of 0.1 to prevent divide-by-zero errors.
caching_and_validation.memoization: Calculated prices are memoized using cacheKey: [tierId, currencyCode, billingCycle].join('-'). Cache clears on state toggles.
